items.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Jiuzhaigou and Huanglong
items.summary: Flying into the heart of China, Jiuzhaigou, was breathtaking! (and cold), leaving…
items.content_html:
Flying into the heart of China, Jiuzhaigou, was breathtaking! (and cold), leaving the big cities behind and seeing the rocky snow-capped mountains upon arrival I felt like I was in an entirely different place altogether! I had two days here the first day I visited the snow-clad mountains of Huanglong Valley and trekked all the way up to the famous Multi-colored Pond, after coming back down from the mountains I couldn’t find my taxi driver, the nice Malaysian people I was sharing the ride with and my luggage! about an hour later and all was sorted (was starting to get a bit worried for a bit until the drivers mysteriously turned up and Malaysian group started to appear out of the exit) and then it was a gruelling 3/4 hour taxi journey late into the night to the mega busy Jiuzhaigou, and more trouble with my accommodation and nowhere accepting visa cards but that’s another story… next day was spent in what can only be described as one of the most scenic places I’ve ever been to in my life “fullstop”. Needless to say I let the photo’s do the talking from this point…

Huanglong Valley

Jiuzhaigou Photos

items.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Xi’an
items.summary: After my stay in Pingyao I caught another bullet train to Xi’an,…
items.content_html:
After my stay in Pingyao I caught another bullet train to Xi’an, home of The Terracotta Army! I’ve seen this place on TV quite a few times but nothing can prepare you for the first time you see it in real life ~ just wow, the size of this place is incredible, as you can see in the photo it’s of stadium proportions. I also hired a bike and rode along the City Wall of Xian, got some good snaps when dusk fell too!

General Xi'an Photos

The Terracotta Army Photos

City Wall of Xian Photos

items.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Pingyao
items.summary: After a bit of trouble with the trains (not knowing Beijing had…
items.content_html:
After a bit of trouble with the trains (not knowing Beijing had no less than four main railway stations, I went to catch my bullet train to Pingyao from the wrong one and consequently missed it, luckily not only did I get a full refund but also the next train a few hours later on that same day – phew…) I arrived in smoggy Pingyao! This really felt like old China (this city dates back some 2,700 years) and staying in a quaint hotel in the middle of the city was surreal, I don’t think I saw even one westerner at this place! I took a walk along the City walls & checked out banking buildings from the past ~ Pingyao was the financial centre of China back in the day. On my leaving I also took a trip to Shuanglin Temple to see The thousand-armed Guanyin!

Pingyao Photos

Shuanglin Temple Photos

items.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Beijing – Part Two
items.summary: On day three in Beijing I checked out a place known as 798…
items.content_html:
On day three in Beijing I checked out a place known as 798 Art Zone or 798 Art District if you’re a local, and as you can see it’s jam-packed full of some the coolest sculptures on the planet, not only this but the whole site used to be a military factory about 50 years ago leaving some great historic buildings dotted around to snap away with your camera (there’s even an old locomotive!). I also visited the Summer Palace unfortunately but not uncommon here, the smog had got really bad so proved difficult to get the snaps I envisioned but still got a few lucky breaks!

798 Art Zone Photos

General Beijing, Hutongs & Summer Palace Photos

items.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Beijing – Part One
items.summary: I catch a bullet train from Shanghai to Beijing and on my…
items.content_html:
I catch a bullet train from Shanghai to Beijing and on my second day I take the advice of a Chinese passenger from the train who told me “Don’t waste the clear skies!” so I arrange a day trip with a taxi driver to The Great Wall of China, the Mutianyu section which I read is less busy than the popular Badaling part, and was rewarded with clear skies and virtually having the wall to myself! In the next few days I also visited The Forbidden City, Beijing Olympic Park, The Temple of Heaven, The CCTV headquarters building and Beihai Park.

The Great Wall of China, Mutianyu Section Photos

The Forbidden City Photos

The Temple of Heaven & Beihai Park Photos

Beijing Olympic Park & The CCTV Headquarters Building Photos

items.title: The Chinese Adventure 2014: Shanghai
items.summary: After a 12 hour flight from Heathrow to Hong Kong, then a…
items.content_html: After a 12 hour flight from Heathrow to Hong Kong, then a connecting 2 hour flight to Shanghai I start my China adventure by taking the Maglev train (which uses magnetic levitation to get along with a top speed of 430km/h) into the city and for the next couple of days visit sights such as The Bund Waterfront, The Old Town & Yu Yuan Garden, the view at night from the World Financial Center and I also took part in a Traditional Tea Ceremony!
